narrative:
The weather forecast is becoming easier, drier, and generally more pleasant with the passage of a cold front with only an isolated shower risk. We have just a few nuisance items to tend to... Spotty light rain is possible late Friday night into Saturday south of Avery County, and a renegade shower cannot be ruled out late Sunday night into Monday morning. Otherwise, expect pleasant weather this weekend and through most of next week. Nights will be rather cool beginning Saturday night.

2027 Blue Ridge Cameo Photo Winners Announced

The second set of winning photos, the "Cameo Winners," has been announced. We receive so many amazing photos in the Photo Contest (851 this year), choosing only 15 winners is just not enough to tell the story. So, we feature an additional 36 "Cameos" throughout the calendar. See our Photo Contest page to see this second set of winning photos.

Forecast Discussion

We are headed into a significant pattern shift. High pressure, that has been in the Western Atlantic for many days, is vacationing in the Western Caribbean for the next week or longer. A trough digs into the northeastern U.S. and Mid-Atlantic — we are on the southwest fringe of this feature.

At the surface, a front pushes south of us today — the leading edge of this change. Saturday, a disturbance moves along the front from west to east. And a secondary front arrives Monday.

High pressure centered in eastern Canada and the Great Lakes will be in control of our weather. We are far enough from the center of that feature that daytime temperatures are not affected that much compared to yesterday, but the air is drier and you will feel that change.

We contend with three brushes with showers, mostly "chin music." With the frontal passage, just an isolated shower is possible. With the low to our south Saturday, areas south of Avery County could see some spotty light rain late Friday night into Saturday. And with the secondary front approaching Monday, I cannot completely rule out a stray shower late Sunday night into Monday.

Otherwise we have mighty fine weather for the end of May and start of June. Daytimes are pleasant, but nights are cool beginning Saturday night.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for May 25, 2026

It's the Memorial Day Monday Message, where we recap our sorely-needed drought relief and discuss our region's cyclical warm/cool periods.

May 25, 2026
Rainy, Cool Saturday

It's a rainy, cool Saturday around western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia. High temperatures are running as much as 20-degrees below normal in the heart of the cold air damming zone.

Sunday and Memorial Day will continue to be unsettled, but temperatures will moderate and there will be additional breaks in the clouds, as the rainfall pattern becomes more convective, or showery in nature.

While the rainfall is falling on our holiday weekend, it's entirely beneficial with respect to our long-standing drought conditions.
